back_translation: The Lord spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,

A person, when there is in the skin of his flesh a swelling, or rash, or bright spot, and it becomes in the skin of his flesh an infection of skin disease, he shall be brought to Aaron the priest or to one of his sons, the priests.

And the priest shall examine the infection on the skin of the flesh, and if the hair in the infection has turned white and the appearance of the infection is deeper than the skin of his flesh, it is an infection of skin disease; and the priest shall examine him and pronounce him unclean.

And if it is a white bright spot on the skin of his flesh, and its appearance is not deeper than the skin, and its hair has not turned white, then the priest shall isolate the infection for seven days.

And the priest shall examine him on the seventh day, and behold, if the infection has remained in his sight—the infection has not spread on the skin—the priest shall isolate him for seven days a second time.

And the priest shall examine him on the seventh day a second time, and behold, the infection has faded, and the infection has not spread on the skin—the priest shall pronounce him clean; it is a rash. And he shall wash his clothes and shall be clean.
But if the rash truly spreads on the skin after he has been shown to the priest for his cleansing, he shall be shown a second time to the priest.
And the priest shall examine him, and behold, the rash has spread on the skin, and the priest shall pronounce him unclean; it is a skin disease.
When an infection of skin disease is on a person, he shall be brought to the priest.
And the priest shall examine him, and behold, a white swelling on the skin and it has turned the hair white, and there is raw living flesh in the swelling—
It is an old skin disease in the skin of his flesh, and the priest shall pronounce him unclean. He shall not isolate him, for he is unclean.
And if the skin disease truly breaks out on the skin, and the skin disease covers all the skin of the infection from his head to his feet, according to all the view of the eyes of the priest,
And the priest shall examine, and behold, the skin disease has covered all his flesh, and he shall pronounce the infection clean—it has all turned white; he is clean.
And on the day raw living flesh appears in him, he shall be unclean.
And the priest shall examine the raw living flesh and pronounce him unclean; the raw living flesh is unclean—it is a skin disease.
Or when the raw living flesh returns and turns white, he shall come to the priest.
And the priest shall examine him, and behold, the infection has turned white—the priest shall pronounce the infection clean; he is clean.
And the flesh, when there is in him, in his skin, a boil, and it heals—
And in the place of the boil there is a white swelling or a reddish-white bright spot, and he shall be shown to the priest.

And the priest shall examine, and behold, its appearance is deeper than the skin, and its hair has turned white—and the priest shall pronounce him unclean; it is an infection of skin disease that has broken out in the boil.
And if the priest examines it, and behold, there is no white hair in it, and it is not lower than the skin, and it has faded, the priest shall isolate him for seven days.
And if it truly spreads in the skin, the priest shall pronounce him unclean; it is an infection.
But if under it the bright spot remains—it has not spread—it is the scar of the boil, and the priest shall pronounce him clean.
Or flesh, when there is in its skin a burn of fire, and the raw living flesh of the burn becomes a bright spot reddish-white or white,

And the priest shall examine it, and behold, the hair has turned white in the bright spot, and its appearance is deeper than the skin—it is a skin disease that has broken out in the burn, and the priest shall pronounce him unclean; it is an infection of skin disease.
And if the priest examines it, and behold, there is no white hair in the bright spot, and it is not lower than the skin, and it has faded, the priest shall isolate him for seven days.
And the priest shall examine him on the seventh day; if it truly spreads in the skin, the priest shall pronounce him unclean; it is an infection of skin disease.
And if under it the bright spot remains—it has not spread on the skin and it has faded—it is the swelling of the burn, and the priest shall pronounce him clean, for it is the scar of the burn.
And a man or a woman, when there is in him an infection on the head or on the chin—

And the priest shall examine the infection, and behold, its appearance is deeper than the skin, and in it is yellow thin hair—and the priest shall pronounce him unclean; it is a scaly outbreak, a skin disease of the head or the chin.

And when the priest examines the scaly outbreak, and behold, its appearance is not deeper than the skin, and there is no black hair in it—then the priest shall isolate the scaly outbreak for seven days.

And the priest shall examine the infection on the seventh day, and behold, the scaly outbreak has not spread, and there is no yellow hair in it, and the appearance of the scaly outbreak is not deeper than the skin—
Then he shall shave himself, but he shall not shave the scaly outbreak, and the priest shall isolate the scaly outbreak for seven days a second time.

And the priest shall examine the scaly outbreak on the seventh day, and behold, the scaly outbreak has not spread in the skin, and its appearance is not deeper than the skin—the priest shall pronounce him clean, and he shall wash his clothes, and he shall be clean.
And if the scaly outbreak truly spreads in the skin after his cleansing—
And the priest shall examine him, and behold, the scaly outbreak has spread in the skin—the priest need not search for the yellow hair; he is unclean.
And if in his sight the scaly outbreak remained, and black hair has grown in it—the scaly outbreak has healed; he is clean, and the priest shall pronounce him clean.
And a man or woman, when there is in the skin of their flesh bright spots, white bright spots—
And the priest shall examine, and behold, in the skin of their flesh are dull-white bright spots—it is a harmless rash that has broken out on the skin; he is clean.
And a man, when his head loses hair, he is bald; he is clean.
And if from the front of his face his head loses hair, he is bald on the forehead; he is clean.
And when there is on the bald head or on the bald forehead a reddish-white infection—it is a skin disease breaking out on his bald head or on his bald forehead.
And the priest shall examine him, and behold, the swelling of the infection is reddish-white on his bald head or on his bald forehead, like the appearance of skin disease of the flesh-skin—
He is a diseased man; he is unclean. The priest shall surely pronounce him unclean; his infection is on his head.
And the diseased person who has the infection—his clothes shall be torn, and his head shall be let loose, and he shall cover his lip and cry, 'Unclean, unclean!'
All the days the infection is in him he shall be unclean; he is unclean. He shall dwell alone; outside the camp his dwelling shall be.
And a garment, when there is in it an infection of skin disease—in a wool garment or in a linen garment—
Or in the warp or in the woof, of linen or of wool, or in leather, or in any work of leather—
And the infection is green or red, in the garment or in the leather, or in the warp or in the woof, or in any leather article—it is an infection of skin disease, and it shall be shown to the priest.
And the priest shall examine the infection and isolate the infection for seven days.

And he shall examine the infection on the seventh day; if the infection has spread in the garment or in the warp or in the woof, or in the leather, for everything that the leather is used for in work—it is a harmful skin disease; the infection is unclean.
And he shall burn the garment or the warp or the woof, in wool or in linen, or any leather article in which the infection is, for it is a harmful skin disease; in fire it shall be burned.
And if the priest examines, and behold, the infection has not spread in the garment or in the warp or in the woof or in any leather article—
And the priest shall command, and they shall wash that in which is the infection, and he shall isolate it seven days a second time.

And the priest shall examine after the infection has been washed, and behold, the infection has not changed its appearance, and the infection has not spread; it is unclean—in fire you shall burn it. It is a hollow on its back-side or its front-side.
And if the priest examines, and behold, the infection has faded after it has been washed, then he shall tear it out from the garment or from the leather or from the warp or from the woof.
And if it appears again in the garment or in the warp or in the woof, or in any leather article—it is spreading; in fire you shall burn that in which is the infection.
And the garment or the warp or the woof, or any leather article that you wash, and the infection departs from them—it shall be washed a second time, and shall be clean.
This is the law of the infection of skin disease—a garment of wool or of linen, or the warp or the woof, or any leather article—to pronounce it clean or to pronounce it unclean.
